zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Mortimer於1190年出生在英國賀福爾維格莫時，她的父親Sir Roger Mortimer Lord of Wigmor32歲，母親Isabella de Ferrers24歲。她於1212年與Walter de Beauchamp在英國武斯特斯爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於大約1268年在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾艾爾姆利 卡素享年79歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Mortimer于1190年出生在英国賀福爾維格莫时，她的父亲Sir Roger Mortimer Lord of Wigmor32岁，母亲Isabella de Ferrers24岁。她于1212年与Walter de Beauchamp在英国武斯特斯爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于大约1268年在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾艾爾姆利 卡素享年79岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Mortimer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Mortemer in Seine-Maritime, France, so called from Old French mort(e) ‘dead’ + mer ‘sea’, probably referring to a stagnant pond or drained swamp.

Irish (of Norman origin): the Norman surname in sense 1 above was taken to Ireland from England in the medieval period, where it has also been adopted by bearers of the Gaelic surnames Mac Muircheartaigh and ÓMuircheartaigh, commonly Anglicized as McMurty and Mortagh. Compare McMurdo .

variants.name: Mortimore
meanings: English (Devon; of Norman origin): variant of   Mortimer  .

variants.name: McMurdo
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muircheartaigh or Mac Muircheartaich, ‘son of Muircheartach’, a personal name meaning ‘sea-ruler’. In Scotland the name was often lenited to give McCurdy . Compare Murdoch and Moriarty . The name was also used to Anglicize Mac Murchaidh (see McMorrow ).

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Mortimer was born in 1190, in Wigmore, Herefordshire, England, her father, Sir Roger Mortimer Lord of Wigmor, was 32 and her mother, Isabella de Ferrers, was 24. She married Walter de Beauchamp in 1212, in Worcestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died about 1268, in Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Mortimer est née en 1190, à Wigmore, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Roger Mortimer Lord of Wigmor, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Isabella de Ferrers, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Walter de Beauchamp en 1212, à Worcestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée environ 1268, à Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 79 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Mortimerが1190年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアウィグモーで生まれた時，父Sir Roger Mortimer Lord of Wigmorは32歳，母Isabella de Ferrersは24歳であった。1212年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアでWalter de Beauchampと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は約1268年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアエルムリー カストルで79歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Mortimer(이)가 1190년 영국 허퍼드실르 위그모어에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Roger Mortimer Lord of Wigmor(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabella de Ferrers(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1212년 영국 워스스터실르에서 Walter de Beauchamp(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1268년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 엘리 카스틀에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Walter de Beauchamp em 1212, em Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Mortimer родилась в 1190 г. в местности вигмор, хирефордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Roger Mortimer Lord of Wigmor было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Isabella de Ferrers, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Walter de Beauchamp в 1212 г. в местности ворсестиршир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла около 1268 г. в местности элмли кастл, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 79 лет.
